records.ety: [OE. sop, soppe; akin to AS. s<?/pan to sup, to sip, to drink, D. sop sop, G. suppe soup, Icel. soppa sop. See Sup, v. t., and cf. Soup.]

defs: Anything steeped, or dipped and softened, in any liquid; especially, something dipped in broth or liquid food, and intended to be eaten.
quotes: He it is to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it.
Sops in wine, quantity, inebriate more than wine itself.
The bounded waters Should lift their bosoms higher than the shores, And make a sop of all this solid globe.

defs: Anything given to pacify; -- so called from the sop given to Cerberus, as related in mythology.
quotes: All nature is cured with a sop.

defs: A thing of little or no value.
quotes: Garlands of roses and sops in wine.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sopped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sopping.]

defs: To steep or dip in any liquid.
